name: CHANCE
fullName: Clopidogrel with Aspirin in Acute Minor Stroke or TIA
population: n=5170, ≥40, NIHSS NIHSS ≤3 (or high-risk TIA), ≤24 h
teachingPoint: Founding evidence for short-course DAPT in minor stroke / high-risk TIA.

name: POINT
fullName: Clopidogrel and Aspirin in Acute Ischemic Stroke and High-Risk TIA
teachingPoint: Reinforces 21-day cap on DAPT for minor stroke.

name: THALES
fullName: Ticagrelor and Aspirin or Aspirin Alone in AIS or TIA
teachingPoint: Supports ticagrelor-based DAPT, especially when CYP2C19 LOF suspected.

name: INSPIRES
fullName: Dual Antiplatelet Treatment up to 72 Hours after Ischemic Stroke
population: n=6100, ≥35, NIHSS mild stroke or high-risk TIA, ≤72 h
teachingPoint: Allows DAPT initiation up to 72 h in eligible mild stroke.

name: CHANCE-2
fullName: Ticagrelor or Clopidogrel with Aspirin in High-Risk TIA or Minor Stroke
teachingPoint: Supports ticagrelor over clopidogrel in CYP2C19 LOF carriers.

name: TAPIS
fullName: Ticagrelor with Aspirin Dual Antiplatelet Therapy combined with Intravenous Thrombolysis in Patients with Ischaemic Stroke in China
population: n=1382, Median 65.6, NIHSS 4-10, ≤6 h of onset
teachingPoint: Early oral DAPT within 6 h as an adjunct to thrombolysis improved excellent outcomes in moderate stroke — a strategy long considered contraindicated; small sICH risk not excluded.
